text:
EXPLANATION: The paper "Model soups: averaging weights of multiple fine-tuned models improves accuracy without increasing inference time" is a real, widely cited publication from the 39th International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML) in 2022. The cited reference is accurate; the minor variations in the title and the inclusion of "et al" instead of the full author list are common citation practices.

LINK: https://proceedings.mlr.press/v162/wortsman22a.html
FOUND_TITLE: Model soups: averaging weights of multiple fine-tuned models improves accuracy without increasing inference time
FOUND_AUTHORS: Mitchell Wortsman, Gabriel Ilharco, Samir Yitzhak Gadre, Rebecca Roelofs, Raphael Gontijo-Lopes, Ari S. Morcos, Hongseok Namkoong, Ali Farhadi, Yair Carmon, Simon Kornblith, Ludwig Schmidt
FOUND_YEAR: 2022
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
